summary: Unmerged/superseded HSD 3AA7 decomp PR centered on sysdolphin baselib memory-card/CARD file-block handling. The diff rewrote fn_803AC3F8 to reach a bot-reported 100% match, added large partial C implementations for fn_803ADF90, fn_803AE7F8, and fn_803B0E9C, refined hsd_3AA7 header prototypes and CardState-like layouts, and made mostly style/formatting edits in psdisp, gm, and gr files. decomp.dev reported +352 matched bytes but also a fn_803B21E8 regression; the PR was closed without merge after a comment saying it was superseded by PR #2512.

key_files.evidence: 845 additions / 83 deletions; added CardStateExt/HsdCmdEntry-related local types, rewrote fn_803AC3F8, added fn_803ADF90, fn_803AE7F8, fn_803B0E9C bodies, and changed fn_803B21E8 typing/field access.
Changed hsd_803AAA48/fn_803AD16C away from UNK declarations and gave several fn_803ADF90-family functions state/file_id/data/async/callback-style prototypes.
275 additions / 102 deletions, largely line wrapping in psDispParticles plus a small include reorder; no decomp.dev match change was attributed to psdisp.
Adjusted GXColor compound literal spacing in fn_8018DF68.
Adjusted function-pointer cast spacing in gmClassic_801B3500.
Adjusted function-pointer cast spacing in grIceMt_801FA364.

what_changed: Implemented or partially implemented hsd_3AA7 memory-card routines fn_803ADF90, fn_803AE7F8, and fn_803B0E9C, replacing nearby placeholder comments in the diff.
Reworked fn_803AC3F8 from a larger manual zero-fill/unroll shape into a simple three-entry loop plus tail-zero loop; decomp.dev reported it improved from 82.55% to 100.00% (+62 bytes).
Added hsd_3B2B.h include and used hsd_803B2B20 in fn_803B0E9C digest/checksum-like block handling.
Introduced local layout helpers/types such as HsdCmdEntry, Hsd803B2550Ctx, CardStateExt, and changed CardQueueEntry.x4 from s32 to void* with explicit casts where needed.

Added sync/async CARD I/O logic in the new functions: OSDisableInterrupts/OSRestoreInterrupts busy checks, CARDFastOpen/CARDRead/CARDWrite/CARDClose retry loops, block maps, sequence checks, and queued s32 cmd[9] operations through fn_803AC168.
Changed fn_803B21E8 signature from raw s32 card_state to struct hsd_803AC3E0_arg0_t* and updated command/entry payloads to use card_state->x0; decomp.dev reported this item regressed by 8 bytes.
Updated hsd_3AA7.h prototypes to use typed CardState or hsd_803AC3E0_arg0_t pointers and more descriptive argument names for state/file_id/data/async/callback on several functions.
Applied formatting-only changes to compound literals/function pointer casts in gm_18A5, gmclassic, and gricemt, and extensive line wrapping in psdisp.
smart_moves: The fn_803AC3F8 rewrite demonstrates that removing artificial unrolled zeroing can improve MWCC matching; the simpler loop matched fully according to the bot.
Using a CardStateExt overlay with offset-named arrays x28/x4C/x70/x170/x270 allowed the author to express block/file metadata while preserving unknown layout gaps with pad fields.

The new hsd_3AA7 code models both synchronous direct CARD calls and asynchronous command-queue paths in the same routines, matching the dispatcher-style structure suggested by fn_803AC168 and numeric command buffers.
The added prototypes in hsd_3AA7.h made call sites more type-aware, especially for state pointers and callback/data parameters, reducing reliance on raw s32/UNK declarations.
The implementation keeps repeated CARD API retry loops local and explicit, which is useful evidence for future matching of adjacent memory-card functions.
The diff isolates unrelated style churn to obvious formatting changes, making the substantive hsd_3AA7 matching work easier to identify despite the large line count.
decomp_lessons: For hsd_3AA7/CARD work, struct overlays with known offsets plus pad arrays are a practical bridge between raw decomp and final names; keep fields like x4C/x170/x270 until semantics are proven.

Typed prototypes can improve readability and call-site correctness, but they can also perturb codegen: fn_803B21E8's pointer signature change coincided with a bot-reported regression from 94.85% to 92.73%.
When matching MWCC, prefer the simplest C shape that produces the observed code before adding manual unrolls; fn_803AC3F8 became a full match after simplifying the zero-fill path.

Async HSD card commands in this area appear as 9-word stack buffers with cmd[0] opcodes and state/data/offset/size slots; future decomp should reuse this command-buffer pattern but avoid assigning semantic opcode names until confirmed.
The unusual expression in fn_803AC3F8, `>> ((0, 0x10))`, is likely a codegen/matching nudge; reuse only with local assembly confirmation rather than as normal style.
Because this PR was closed unmerged and superseded by #2512, treat its exact code as intermediate evidence and verify against the accepted successor before copying implementation details.
naming_conventions: Address-based function names remain dominant: fn_803AC3F8, fn_803ADF90, fn_803AE7F8, fn_803B0E9C, fn_803B21E8, hsd_803AAA48.
Temporary/local type names combine subsystem or address hints with generic roles: CardBufEntry, CardQueueEntry, HsdCmdEntry, Hsd803B2550Ctx, CardStateExt.
Unknown struct members stayed offset-style with x-prefixed names and pads: x0, x4, x8, x24, x4C, x170, x270, x3B0, pad_94, pad_3B1.
Header prototypes began moving from raw s32/UNK_PARAMS toward semantic argument names such as state, file_id, data, async, and callback.

Style convention in nearby gm/gr cleanup places a space between the pointed-to type and `(*...)` in function pointer casts, e.g. `(u8 (*)(s32, s32, u8))`, and formats compound literals as `(GXColor) { ... }`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: decomp.dev report: overall matched code 67.19%, +0.01%, +352 bytes for GALE01 c14d251 -> 383f9af.
New full match: main/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3AA7 fn_803AC3F8, +62 bytes, 82.55% -> 100.00%.
Regression to investigate: fn_803B21E8 -8 bytes, 94.85% -> 92.73%, near a signature change and card_state->x0 command payload change.

fn_803AC3F8 matching tactics visible in diff include explicit u32 casts before shifts, a simple bounded loop over three metadata entries, a separate tail-zero loop, and the `((0, 0x10))` shift-count expression.
New CARD routines use fixed-size stack arrays `s32 cmd[9] = { 0 }` and pass them to fn_803AC168 with opcodes including 1, 2, 4, 5, 6, 9, and 10.
Sync paths repeatedly retry CARDRead/CARDWrite/CARDFastOpen/CARDClose while result is -1, usually capped at 10 attempts, which may be important for matching control flow.
review_feedback: There were no GitHub review comments and reviews.json/review_comments.json are empty; human rationale is weak because the PR body was empty.
decomp-dev[bot] reported one new match, three unmatched-item improvements, and one unmatched-item regression, all in main/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3AA7.
A later issue comment by jellejurre stated: `Superseded by https://github.com/doldecomp/melee/pull/2512`; the PR was closed without merge.
follow_up_queries: PR #2512 hsd_3AA7 successor final accepted changes
fn_803B21E8 regression card_state pointer x0 command payload
hsd_3AA7 CardStateExt x170 x270 block map sequence numbers
fn_803AC168 cmd[9] opcode mapping hsd_3AA7 async CARD queue
